NKJV.1: “These things I have spoken to you, that you  should not be made to stumble.
NKJV.2: They will put you out of the synagogues; yes, the time is coming  that whoever kills you will think that he offers God service.
NKJV.3: And  these things they will do to you because they have not known the Father nor Me.
NKJV.4: But these things I have told you, that when the time comes, you may remember that I told you of them. And these things I did not say to you at the beginning, because I was with you.
NKJV.5: “But now I  go away to Him who sent Me, and none of you asks Me, ‘Where are You going?’
NKJV.6: But because I have said these things to you,  sorrow has filled your heart.
NKJV.7: Nevertheless I tell you the truth. It is to your advantage that I go away; for if I do not go away, the Helper will not come to you; but  if I depart, I will send Him to you.
NKJV.8: And when He has  come, He will convict the world of sin, and of righteousness, and of judgment:
NKJV.9: of sin, because they do not believe in Me;
NKJV.10: of righteousness,  because I go to My Father and you see Me no more;
NKJV.11: of judgment, because  the ruler of this world is judged.
NKJV.12: “I still have many things to say to you, but you cannot bear them now.
NKJV.13: However, when He, the Spirit of truth, has come, He will guide you into all truth; for He will not speak on His own authority, but whatever He hears He will speak; and He will tell you things to come.
NKJV.14: He will glorify Me, for He will take of what is Mine and declare it to you.
NKJV.15: All things that the Father has are Mine. Therefore I said that He will take of Mine and declare it to you.
NKJV.16: “A  little while, and you will not see Me; and again a little while, and you will see Me,  because I go to the Father.”
NKJV.17: Then some of His disciples said among themselves, “What is this that He says to us, ‘A little while, and you will not see Me; and again a little while, and you will see Me’; and, ‘because I go to the Father’?”
NKJV.18: They said therefore, “What is this that He says, ‘A little while’? We do not know what He is saying.”
NKJV.19: Now Jesus knew that they desired to ask Him, and He said to them, “Are you inquiring among yourselves about what I said, ‘A little while, and you will not see Me; and again a little while, and you will see Me’?
NKJV.20: Most assuredly, I say to you that you will weep and  lament, but the world will rejoice; and you will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will be turned into  joy.
NKJV.21: A woman, when she is in labor, has sorrow because her hour has come; but as soon as she has given birth to the child, she no longer remembers the anguish, for joy that a human being has been born into the world.
NKJV.22: Therefore you now have sorrow; but I will see you again and  your heart will rejoice, and your joy no one will take from you.
NKJV.23: “And in that day you will ask Me nothing.  Most assuredly, I say to you, whatever you ask the Father in My name He will give you.
NKJV.24: Until now you have asked nothing in My name. Ask, and you will receive,  that your joy may be  full.
NKJV.25: “These things I have spoken to you in figurative language; but the time is coming when I will no longer speak to you in figurative language, but I will tell you  plainly about the Father.
NKJV.26: In that day you will ask in My name, and I do not say to you that I shall pray the Father for you;
NKJV.27: for the Father Himself loves you, because you have loved Me, and  have believed that I came forth from God.
NKJV.28: I came forth from the Father and have come into the world. Again, I leave the world and go to the Father.”
NKJV.29: His disciples said to Him, “See, now You are speaking plainly, and using no figure of speech!
NKJV.30: Now we are sure that You know all things, and have no need that anyone should question You. By this we believe that You came forth from God.”
NKJV.31: Jesus answered them, “Do you now believe?
NKJV.32: Indeed the hour is coming, yes, has now come, that you will be scattered,  each to his own, and will leave Me alone. And  yet I am not alone, because the Father is with Me.
NKJV.33: These things I have spoken to you, that  in Me you may have peace.  In the world you will have tribulation; but be of good cheer,  I have overcome the world.”

ESV.1: “I have said all these things to you to keep you from falling away.
ESV.2: They will put you out of the synagogues. Indeed, the hour is coming when whoever kills you will think he is offering service to God.
ESV.3: And they will do these things because they have not known the Father, nor me.
ESV.4: But I have said these things to you, that when their hour comes you may remember that I told them to you. The Work of the Holy Spirit “I did not say these things to you from the beginning, because I was with you.
ESV.5: But now I am going to him who sent me, and none of you asks me, ‘Where are you going?’
ESV.6: But because I have said these things to you, sorrow has filled your heart.
ESV.7: Nevertheless, I tell you the truth: it is to your advantage that I go away, for if I do not go away, the Helper will not come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.
ESV.8: And when he comes, he will convict the world concerning sin and righteousness and judgment:
ESV.9: concerning sin, because they do not believe in me;
ESV.10: concerning righteousness, because I go to the Father, and you will see me no longer;
ESV.11: concerning judgment, because the ruler of this world is judged.
ESV.12: “I still have many things to say to you, but you cannot bear them now.
ESV.13: When the Spirit of truth comes, he will guide you into all the truth, for he will not speak on his own authority, but whatever he hears he will speak, and he will declare to you the things that are to come.
ESV.14: He will glorify me, for he will take what is mine and declare it to you.
ESV.15: All that the Father has is mine; therefore I said that he will take what is mine and declare it to you.
ESV.16: “A little while, and you will see me no longer; and again a little while, and you will see me.”
ESV.17: So some of his disciples said to one another, “What is this that he says to us,  ‘A little while, and you will not see me, and again a little while, and you will see me’; and,  ‘because I am going to the Father’?”
ESV.18: So they were saying, “What does he mean by ‘a little while’? We do not know what he is talking about.”
ESV.19: Jesus knew that they wanted to ask him, so he said to them, “Is this what you are asking yourselves, what I meant by saying, ‘A little while and you will not see me, and again a little while and you will see me’?
ESV.20: Truly, truly, I say to you, you will weep and lament, but the world will rejoice. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will turn into joy.
ESV.21: When a woman is giving birth, she has sorrow because her hour has come, but when she has delivered the baby, she no longer remembers the anguish, for joy that a human being has been born into the world.
ESV.22: So also you have sorrow now, but I will see you again, and your hearts will rejoice, and no one will take your joy from you.
ESV.23: In that day you will ask nothing of me. Truly, truly, I say to you, whatever you ask of the Father in my name, he will give it to you.
ESV.24: Until now you have asked nothing in my name. Ask, and you will receive, that your joy may be full.
ESV.25: “I have said these things to you in figures of speech. The hour is coming when I will no longer speak to you in figures of speech but will tell you plainly about the Father.
ESV.26: In that day you will ask in my name, and I do not say to you that I will ask the Father on your behalf;
ESV.27: for the Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and have believed that I came from God.
ESV.28: I came from the Father and have come into the world, and now I am leaving the world and going to the Father.”
ESV.29: His disciples said, “Ah, now you are speaking plainly and not using figurative speech!
ESV.30: Now we know that you know all things and do not need anyone to question you; this is why we believe that you came from God.”
ESV.31: Jesus answered them, “Do you now believe?
ESV.32: Behold, the hour is coming, indeed it has come, when you will be scattered, each to his own home, and will leave me alone. Yet I am not alone, for the Father is with me.
ESV.33: I have said these things to you, that in me you may have peace. In the world you will have tribulation. But take heart; I have overcome the world.”

NASB.1: 'These things I have spoken to you so that you may be kept from stumbling.
NASB.2: 'They will make you outcasts from the synagogue, but an hour is coming for everyone who kills you to think that he is offering service to God.
NASB.3: 'These things they will do because they have not known the Father or Me.
NASB.4: 'But these things I have spoken to you, so that when their hour comes, you may remember that I told you of them. These things I did not say to you at the beginning, because I was with you.
NASB.5: 'But now I am going to Him who sent Me; and none of you asks Me, 'Where are You going?'
NASB.6: 'But because I have said these things to you, sorrow has filled your heart.
NASB.7: 'But I tell you the truth, it is to your advantage that I go away; for if I do not go away, the Helper will not come to you; but if I go, I will send Him to you.
NASB.8: 'And He, when He comes, will convict the world concerning sin and righteousness and judgment;
NASB.9: concerning sin, because they do not believe in Me;
NASB.10: and concerning righteousness, because I go to the Father and you no longer see Me;
NASB.11: and concerning judgment, because the ruler of this world has been judged.
NASB.12: 'I have many more things to say to you, but you cannot bear [them] now.
NASB.13: 'But when He, the Spirit of truth, comes, He will guide you into all the truth; for He will not speak on His own initiative, but whatever He hears, He will speak; and He will disclose to you what is to come.
NASB.14: 'He will glorify Me, for He will take of Mine and will disclose [it] to you.
NASB.15: 'All things that the Father has are Mine; therefore I said that He takes of Mine and will disclose [it] to you.
NASB.16: 'A little while, and you will no longer see Me; and again a little while, and you will see Me.'
NASB.17: [Some] of His disciples then said to one another, 'What is this thing He is telling us, 'A little while, and you will not see Me; and again a little while, and you will see Me'; and, 'because I go to the Father '?'
NASB.18: So they were saying, 'What is this that He says, 'A little while '? We do not know what He is talking about.'
NASB.19: Jesus knew that they wished to question Him, and He said to them, 'Are you deliberating together about this, that I said, 'A little while, and you will not see Me, and again a little while, and you will see Me'?
NASB.20: 'Truly, truly, I say to you, that you will weep and lament, but the world will rejoice; you will grieve, but your grief will be turned into joy.
NASB.21: 'Whenever a woman is in labor she has pain, because her hour has come; but when she gives birth to the child, she no longer remembers the anguish because of the joy that a child has been born into the world.
NASB.22: 'Therefore you too have grief now; but I will see you again, and your heart will rejoice, and no one [will] take your joy away from you.
NASB.23: 'In that day you will not question Me about anything. Truly, truly, I say to you, if you ask the Father for anything in My name, He will give it to you.
NASB.24: 'Until now you have asked for nothing in My name; ask and you will receive, so that your joy may be made full.
NASB.25: 'These things I have spoken to you in figurative language; an hour is coming when I will no longer speak to you in figurative language, but will tell you plainly of the Father.
NASB.26: 'In that day you will ask in My name, and I do not say to you that I will request of the Father on your behalf;
NASB.27: for the Father Himself loves you, because you have loved Me and have believed that I came forth from the Father.
NASB.28: 'I came forth from the Father and have come into the world; I am leaving the world again and going to the Father.'
NASB.29: His disciples said, 'Lo, now You are speaking plainly and are not using a figure of speech.
NASB.30: 'Now we know that You know all things, and have no need for anyone to question You; by this we believe that You came from God.'
NASB.31: Jesus answered them, 'Do you now believe?
NASB.32: 'Behold, an hour is coming, and has [already] come, for you to be scattered, each to his own [home], and to leave Me alone; and [yet] I am not alone, because the Father is with Me.
NASB.33: 'These things I have spoken to you, so that in Me you may have peace. In the world you have tribulation, but take courage; I have overcome the world.'

NLT.1: “I have told you these things so that you won’t abandon your faith.
NLT.2: For you will be expelled from the synagogues, and the time is coming when those who kill you will think they are doing a holy service for God.
NLT.3: This is because they have never known the Father or me.
NLT.4: Yes, I’m telling you these things now, so that when they happen, you will remember my warning. I didn’t tell you earlier because I was going to be with you for a while longer.
NLT.5: “But now I am going away to the one who sent me, and not one of you is asking where I am going.
NLT.6: Instead, you grieve because of what I’ve told you.
NLT.7: But in fact, it is best for you that I go away, because if I don’t, the Advocate won’t come. If I do go away, then I will send him to you.
NLT.8: And when he comes, he will convict the world of its sin, and of God’s righteousness, and of the coming judgment.
NLT.9: The world’s sin is that it refuses to believe in me.
NLT.10: Righteousness is available because I go to the Father, and you will see me no more.
NLT.11: Judgment will come because the ruler of this world has already been judged.
NLT.12: “There is so much more I want to tell you, but you can’t bear it now.
NLT.13: When the Spirit of truth comes, he will guide you into all truth. He will not speak on his own but will tell you what he has heard. He will tell you about the future.
NLT.14: He will bring me glory by telling you whatever he receives from me.
NLT.15: All that belongs to the Father is mine; this is why I said, ‘The Spirit will tell you whatever he receives from me.’
NLT.16: “In a little while you won’t see me anymore. But a little while after that, you will see me again.”
NLT.17: Some of the disciples asked each other, “What does he mean when he says, ‘In a little while you won’t see me, but then you will see me,’ and ‘I am going to the Father’?
NLT.18: And what does he mean by ‘a little while’? We don’t understand.”
NLT.19: Jesus realized they wanted to ask him about it, so he said, “Are you asking yourselves what I meant? I said in a little while you won’t see me, but a little while after that you will see me again.
NLT.20: I tell you the truth, you will weep and mourn over what is going to happen to me, but the world will rejoice. You will grieve, but your grief will suddenly turn to wonderful joy.
NLT.21: It will be like a woman suffering the pains of labor. When her child is born, her anguish gives way to joy because she has brought a new baby into the world.
NLT.22: So you have sorrow now, but I will see you again; then you will rejoice, and no one can rob you of that joy.
NLT.23: At that time you won’t need to ask me for anything. I tell you the truth, you will ask the Father directly, and he will grant your request because you use my name.
NLT.24: You haven’t done this before. Ask, using my name, and you will receive, and you will have abundant joy.
NLT.25: “I have spoken of these matters in figures of speech, but soon I will stop speaking figuratively and will tell you plainly all about the Father.
NLT.26: Then you will ask in my name. I’m not saying I will ask the Father on your behalf,
NLT.27: for the Father himself loves you dearly because you love me and believe that I came from God.
NLT.28: Yes, I came from the Father into the world, and now I will leave the world and return to the Father.”
NLT.29: Then his disciples said, “At last you are speaking plainly and not figuratively.
NLT.30: Now we understand that you know everything, and there’s no need to question you. From this we believe that you came from God.”
NLT.32: But the time is coming — indeed it’s here now — when you will be scattered, each one going his own way, leaving me alone. Yet I am not alone because the Father is with me.
NLT.33: I have told you all this so that you may have peace in me. Here on earth you will have many trials and sorrows. But take heart, because I have overcome the world.”

WEB.1: “I have said these things to you so that you wouldn’t be caused to stumble.
WEB.2: They will put you out of the synagogues. Yes, the time comes that whoever kills you will think that he offers service to God.
WEB.3: They will do these things  because they have not known the Father, nor me.
WEB.4: But I have told you these things, so that when the time comes, you may remember that I told you about them. I didn’t tell you these things from the beginning, because I was with you.
WEB.5: But now I am going to him who sent me, and none of you asks me, ‘Where are you going?’
WEB.6: But because I have told you these things, sorrow has filled your heart.
WEB.7: Nevertheless I tell you the truth: It is to your advantage that I go away, for if I don’t go away, the Counselor won’t come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.
WEB.8: When he has come, he will convict the world about sin, about righteousness, and about judgment;
WEB.9: about sin, because they don’t believe in me;
WEB.10: about righteousness, because I am going to my Father, and you won’t see me any more;
WEB.11: about judgment, because the prince of this world has been judged.
WEB.12: “I have yet many things to tell you, but you can’t bear them now.
WEB.13: However when he, the Spirit of truth, has come, he will guide you into all truth, for he will not speak from himself; but whatever he hears, he will speak. He will declare to you things that are coming.
WEB.14: He will glorify me, for he will take from what is mine, and will declare it to you.
WEB.15: All things whatever the Father has are mine; therefore I said that he takes  of mine, and will declare it to you.
WEB.16: A little while, and you will not see me. Again a little while, and you will see me.”
WEB.17: Some of his disciples therefore said to one another, “What is this that he says to us, ‘A little while, and you won’t see me, and again a little while, and you will see me;’ and, ‘Because I go to the Father’?”
WEB.18: They said therefore, “What is this that he says, ‘A little while’? We don’t know what he is saying.”
WEB.19: Therefore Jesus perceived that they wanted to ask him, and he said to them, “Do you inquire among yourselves concerning this, that I said, ‘A little while, and you won’t see me, and again a little while, and you will see me?’
WEB.20: Most certainly I tell you, that you will weep and lament, but the world will rejoice. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will be turned into joy.
WEB.21: A woman, when she gives birth, has sorrow, because her time has come. But when she has delivered the child, she doesn’t remember the anguish any more, for the joy that a human being is born into the world.
WEB.22: Therefore you now have sorrow, but I will see you again, and your heart will rejoice, and no one will take your joy away from you.
WEB.23: “In that day you will ask me no questions. Most certainly I tell you, whatever you may ask of the Father in my name, he will give it to you.
WEB.24: Until now, you have asked nothing in my name. Ask, and you will receive, that your joy may be made full.
WEB.25: I have spoken these things to you in figures of speech. But the time is coming when I will no more speak to you in figures of speech, but will tell you plainly about the Father.
WEB.26: In that day you will ask in my name; and I don’t say to you, that I will pray to the Father for you,
WEB.27: for the Father himself loves you, because you have loved me, and have believed that I came from God.
WEB.28: I came from the Father, and have come into the world. Again, I leave the world, and go to the Father.”
WEB.29: His disciples said to him, “Behold, now you speak plainly, and speak no figures of speech.
WEB.30: Now we know that you know all things, and don’t need for anyone to question you. By this we believe that you came from God.”
WEB.31: Jesus answered them, “Do you now believe?
WEB.32: Behold, the time is coming, yes, and has now come, that you will be scattered, everyone to his own place, and you will leave me alone. Yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.
WEB.33: I have told you these things, that in me you may have peace. In the world you have oppression; but cheer up! I have overcome the world.”

CSB17.1: “I have told you these things to keep you from stumbling.
CSB17.2: They will ban you from the synagogues. <sup>ⓜ</sup>In fact, a time is coming when anyone who kills you will think he is offering service <sup>ⓝ</sup>to God.
CSB17.3: They will do these things because they haven’t known <sup>ⓐ</sup>the Father or me.
CSB17.4: But I have told you these things so that when their time <sup>[1]</sup>comes you will remember <sup>ⓑ</sup>I told them to you. I didn’t tell you these things from the beginning, because I was with you.
CSB17.5: But now I am going away <sup>ⓒ</sup>to him who sent me, and not one of you asks me, ‘Where are you going?’ <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.6: Yet, because I have spoken these things to you, sorrow has filled your heart.
CSB17.7: Nevertheless, I am telling you the truth. <sup>ⓔ</sup>It is for your benefit that I go away, because if I don’t go away the Counselor will not come to you. If I go, I will send him to you. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.8: When he comes, he will convict the world about sin, righteousness, and judgment:
CSB17.9: About sin, because they do not believe in me;
CSB17.10: about righteousness, because I am going to the Father <sup>ⓖ</sup>and you will no longer see me;
CSB17.11: and about judgment, <sup>ⓗ</sup>because the ruler of this world <sup>ⓘ</sup>has been judged. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.12: “I still have many things to tell you, but you can’t bear them now. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.13: When the Spirit of truth <sup>ⓛ</sup>comes, he will guide you into all the truth. <sup>ⓜ</sup>For he will not speak on his own, but he will speak whatever he hears. He will also declare to you what is to come.
CSB17.14: He will glorify me, because he will take from what is mine and declare it to you.
CSB17.15: Everything the Father <sup>ⓝ</sup>has is mine. <sup>ⓐ</sup>This is why I told you that he takes from what is mine and will declare it to you. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.16: “A little while <sup>ⓒ</sup> and you will no longer see me;  <sup>ⓓ</sup> again a little while and you will see me.” <sup>[2]</sup>
CSB17.17: Then some of his disciples said to one another, “What is this he’s telling us: ‘A little while and you will not see me; again a little while and you will see me’ and, ‘because I am going to the Father’ <sup>ⓔ</sup>?”
CSB17.18: They said, “What is this he is saying, <sup>[3]</sup> ‘A little while’? We don’t know what he’s talking about.”
CSB17.19: Jesus knew they wanted to ask him, and so he said to them, “Are you asking one another about what I said, ‘A little while and you will not see me; again a little while and you will see me’?
CSB17.20: Truly I tell you, you will weep and mourn, <sup>ⓕ</sup>but the world will rejoice. You will become sorrowful, <sup>ⓖ</sup>but your sorrow will turn to joy.
CSB17.21: When a woman is in labor, she has pain <sup>ⓗ</sup>because her time has come. But when she has given birth to a child, <sup>ⓘ</sup>she no longer remembers the suffering because of the joy that a person has been born into the world.
CSB17.22: So you also have sorrow <sup>[4]</sup>now. But I will see you again. Your hearts will rejoice, <sup>ⓙ</sup>and no one will take away your joy from you. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.23: “In that day you will not ask me anything. Truly I tell you, anything you ask the Father in my name, he will give you. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.24: Until now you have asked for nothing in my name. <sup>ⓜ</sup>Ask and you will receive, <sup>ⓝ</sup>so that your joy may be complete. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.25: “I have spoken these things to you in figures of speech. A time is coming when I will no longer speak to you in figures, but I will tell you plainly about the Father.
CSB17.26: On that day you will ask <sup>ⓑ</sup>in my name, and <sup>ⓒ</sup>I am not telling you that I will ask the Father on your behalf.
CSB17.27: For the Father himself loves you, because you have loved <sup>ⓓ</sup>me and have believed that I came from God. <sup>[5]</sup> <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.28: I came from the Father and have come into the world. Again, I am leaving the world and going to the Father.” <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.29: His disciples said, “Look, now you’re speaking plainly and not using any figurative language.
CSB17.30: Now we know that you know everything <sup>ⓖ</sup> and don’t need anyone to question you. By this we believe that you came from God.” <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.31: Jesus responded to them, “Do you now believe?
CSB17.32: Indeed, an hour is coming, and has come, <sup>ⓘ</sup>when each of you will be scattered to his own home, and you will leave me alone. Yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.33: I have told you these things so that in me you may have peace. You will have suffering in this world. Be courageous! I have conquered the world.”

AMP.1: "I have told you these things so that you will not stumble or be caught off guard and fall away.
AMP.2: They will put you out of the synagogues and make you outcasts. And a time is coming when whoever kills you will think that he is offering service to God.
AMP.3: And they will do these things because they have not known the Father or Me.
AMP.4: I have told you these things [now], so that when their time comes, you will remember that I told you about them. I did not say these things to you at the beginning, because I was with you.
AMP.5: "But now I am going to Him who sent Me; and none of you asks Me, 'Where are You going?'
AMP.6: But because I have said these things to you, sorrow has filled your hearts [and taken complete possession of them].
AMP.7: But I tell you the truth, it is to your advantage that I go away; for if I do not go away, the Helper (Comforter, Advocate, Intercessor—Counselor, Strengthener, Standby) will not come to you; but if I go, I will send Him (the Holy Spirit) to you [to be in close fellowship with you].
AMP.8: And He, when He comes, will convict the world about [the guilt of] sin [and the need for a Savior], and about righteousness, and about judgment:
AMP.9: about sin [and the true nature of it], because they do not believe in Me [and My message];
AMP.10: about righteousness [personal integrity and godly character], because I am going to My Father and you will no longer see Me;
AMP.11: about judgment [the certainty of it], because the ruler of this world (Satan) has been judged and condemned.
AMP.12: "I have many more things to say to you, but you cannot bear [to hear] them now.
AMP.13: But when He, the Spirit of Truth, comes, He will guide you into all the truth [full and complete truth]. For He will not speak on His own initiative, but He will speak whatever He hears [from the Father—the message regarding the Son], and He will disclose to you what is to come [in the future].
AMP.14: He will glorify and honor Me, because He (the Holy Spirit) will take from what is Mine and will disclose it to you.
AMP.15: All things that the Father has are Mine. Because of this I said that He [the Spirit] will take from what is Mine and will reveal it to you.
AMP.16: "A little while, and you will no longer see Me; and again a little while, and you will see Me."
AMP.17: Some of His disciples said to one another, "What does He mean when He tells us, 'A little while, and you will not see Me; and again a little while, and you will see Me'; and, 'because I am going to My Father'?"
AMP.18: So they were saying, "What does He mean when He says, 'A little while'? We do not know what He is talking about."
AMP.19: Jesus knew that they wanted to ask Him, so He said to them, "Are you wondering among yourselves about what I meant when I said, 'A little while, and you will not see Me, and again a little while, and you will see Me'?
AMP.20: I assure you and most solemnly say to you, that you will weep and grieve [in great mourning], but the world will rejoice. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will be turned into joy.
AMP.21: A woman, when she is in labor, has pain because her time [to give birth] has come; but when she has given birth to the child, she no longer remembers the anguish because of her joy that a child has come into the world.
AMP.22: So for now you are in grief; but I will see you again, and [then] your hearts will rejoice, and no one will take away from you your [great] joy.
AMP.23: In that day you will not [need to] ask Me about anything. I assure you and most solemnly say to you, whatever you ask the Father in My name [as My representative], He will give you.
AMP.24: Until now you have not asked [the Father] for anything in My name; but now ask and keep on asking and you will receive, so that your joy may be full and complete.
AMP.25: "I have told you these things in figurative language (veiled language, proverbs); the hour is now coming when I will no longer speak to you in figures of speech, but I will tell you plainly about the Father.
AMP.26: In that day you will ask in My name, and I am not saying to you that I will ask the Father on your behalf [because it will be unnecessary];
AMP.27: for the Father Himself [tenderly] loves you, because you have loved Me and have believed that I came from the Father.
AMP.28: I came from the Father and have come into the world; again, I am leaving the world and going to the Father."
AMP.29: His disciples said, "Ah, now You are speaking plainly to us and not in figures of speech!
AMP.30: Now we know that You know all things, and have no need for anyone to question You; because of this we believe [without any doubt] that you came from God."
AMP.31: Jesus answered them, "Do you now [at last] believe?
AMP.32: Take careful notice: an hour is coming, and has arrived, when you will all be scattered, each to his own home, leaving Me alone; and yet I am not alone, because the Father is with Me.
AMP.33: I have told you these things, so that in Me you may have [perfect] peace. In the world you have tribulation and distress and suffering, but be courageous [be confident, be undaunted, be filled with joy]; I have overcome the world." [My conquest is accomplished, My victory abiding.]

MSG.1: "I've told you these things to prepare you for rough times ahead.
MSG.2: They are going to throw you out of the meeting places. There will even come a time when anyone who kills you will think he's doing God a favor.
MSG.3: They will do these things because they never really understood the Father.
MSG.4: I've told you these things so that when the time comes and they start in on you, you'll be well-warned and ready for them. "I didn't tell you this earlier because I was with you every day.
MSG.5: But now I am on my way to the One who sent me. Not one of you has asked, 'Where are you going?'
MSG.6: Instead, the longer I've talked, the sadder you've become.
MSG.7: So let me say it again, this truth: It's better for you that I leave. If I don't leave, the Friend won't come. But if I go, I'll send him to you.
MSG.8: "When he comes, he'll expose the error of the godless world's view of sin, righteousness, and judgment:
MSG.9: He'll show them that their refusal to believe in me is their basic sin;
MSG.10: that righteousness comes from above, where I am with the Father, out of their sight and control;
MSG.11: that judgment takes place as the ruler of this godless world is brought to trial and convicted.
MSG.12: "I still have many things to tell you, but you can't handle them now.
MSG.13: But when the Friend comes, the Spirit of the Truth, he will take you by the hand and guide you into all the truth there is. He won't draw attention to himself, but will make sense out of what is about to happen and, indeed, out of all that I have done and said.
MSG.14: He will honor me; he will take from me and deliver it to you.
MSG.15: Everything the Father has is also mine. That is why I've said, 'He takes from me and delivers to you.'
MSG.16: "In a day or so you're not going to see me, but then in another day or so you will see me."
MSG.17: That stirred up a hornet's nest of questions among the disciples: "What's he talking about: 'In a day or so you're not going to see me, but then in another day or so you will see me'? And, 'Because I'm on my way to the Father'?
MSG.18: What is this 'day or so'? We don't know what he's talking about."
MSG.19: Jesus knew they were dying to ask him what he meant, so he said, "Are you trying to figure out among yourselves what I meant when I said, 'In a day or so you're not going to see me, but then in another day or so you will see me'?
MSG.20: Then fix this firmly in your minds: You're going to be in deep mourning while the godless world throws a party. You'll be sad, very sad, but your sadness will develop into gladness.
MSG.21: "When a woman gives birth, she has a hard time, there's no getting around it. But when the baby is born, there is joy in the birth. This new life in the world wipes out memory of the pain.
MSG.22: The sadness you have right now is similar to that pain, but the coming joy is also similar. When I see you again, you'll be full of joy, and it will be a joy no one can rob from you.
MSG.23: You'll no longer be so full of questions. "This is what I want you to do: Ask the Father for whatever is in keeping with the things I've revealed to you.
MSG.24: Ask in my name, according to my will, and he'll most certainly give it to you. Your joy will be a river overflowing its banks!
MSG.25: "I've used figures of speech in telling you these things. Soon I'll drop the figures and tell you about the Father in plain language.
MSG.26: Then you can make your requests directly to him in relation to this life I've revealed to you. I won't continue making requests of the Father on your behalf.
MSG.27: I won't need to. Because you've gone out on a limb, committed yourselves to love and trust in me, believing I came directly from the Father, the Father loves you directly.
MSG.28: First, I left the Father and arrived in the world; now I leave the world and travel to the Father."
MSG.29: His disciples said, "Finally! You're giving it to us straight, in plain talk—no more figures of speech.
MSG.30: Now we know that you know everything—it all comes together in you. You won't have to put up with our questions anymore. We're convinced you came from God."
MSG.31: Jesus answered them, "Do you finally believe?
MSG.32: In fact, you're about to make a run for it—saving your own skins and abandoning me. But I'm not abandoned. The Father is with me.
MSG.33: I've told you all this so that trusting me, you will be unshakable and assured, deeply at peace. In this godless world you will continue to experience difficulties. But take heart! I've conquered the world."

NIV.1: “All this I have told you so that you will not go astray.
NIV.2: They will put you out of the synagogue; in fact, a time is coming when anyone who kills you will think he is offering a service to God.
NIV.3: They will do such things because they have not known the Father or me.
NIV.4: I have told you this, so that when the time comes you will remember that I warned you. I did not tell you this at first because I was with you.
NIV.5:
The Work of the Holy Spirit
“Now I am going to him who sent me, yet none of you asks me, ‘Where are you going?’
NIV.6: Because I have said these things, you are filled with grief.
NIV.7: But I tell you the truth: It is for your good that I am going away. Unless I go away, the Counselor will not come to you; but if I go, I will send him to you.
NIV.8: When he comes, he will convict the world of guilt in regard to sin and righteousness and judgment:
NIV.9: in regard to sin, because men do not believe in me;
NIV.10: in regard to righteousness, because I am going to the Father, where you can see me no longer;
NIV.11: and in regard to judgment, because the prince of this world now stands condemned.
NIV.12: “I have much more to say to you, more than you can now bear.
NIV.13: But when he, the Spirit of truth, comes, he will guide you into all truth. He will not speak on his own; he will speak only what he hears, and he will tell you what is yet to come.
NIV.14: He will bring glory to me by taking from what is mine and making it known to you.
NIV.15: All that belongs to the Father is mine. That is why I said the Spirit will take from what is mine and make it known to you.
NIV.16: “In a little while you will see me no more, and then after a little while you will see me.”
NIV.17:
The Disciples’ Grief Will Turn to Joy
Some of his disciples said to one another, “What does he mean by saying, ‘In a little while you will see me no more, and then after a little while you will see me,’ and ‘Because I am going to the Father’?”
NIV.18: They kept asking, “What does he mean by ‘a little while’? We don’t understand what he is saying.”
NIV.19: Jesus saw that they wanted to ask him about this, so he said to them, “Are you asking one another what I meant when I said, ‘In a little while you will see me no more, and then after a little while you will see me’?
NIV.20: I tell you the truth, you will weep and mourn while the world rejoices. You will grieve, but your grief will turn to joy.
NIV.21: A woman giving birth to a child has pain because her time has come; but when her baby is born she forgets the anguish because of her joy that a child is born into the world.
NIV.22: So with you: Now is your time of grief, but I will see you again and you will rejoice, and no one will take away your joy.
NIV.23: In that day you will no longer ask me anything. I tell you the truth, my Father will give you whatever you ask in my name.
NIV.24: Until now you have not asked for anything in my name. Ask and you will receive, and your joy will be complete.
NIV.25: “Though I have been speaking figuratively, a time is coming when I will no longer use this kind of language but will tell you plainly about my Father.
NIV.26: In that day you will ask in my name. I am not saying that I will ask the Father on your behalf.
NIV.27: No, the Father himself loves you because you have loved me and have believed that I came from God.
NIV.28: I came from the Father and entered the world; now I am leaving the world and going back to the Father.”
NIV.29: Then Jesus’ disciples said, “Now you are speaking clearly and without figures of speech.
NIV.30: Now we can see that you know all things and that you do not even need to have anyone ask you questions. This makes us believe that you came from God.”
NIV.32: “But a time is coming, and has come, when you will be scattered, each to his own home. You will leave me all alone. Yet I am not alone, for my Father is with me.
NIV.33: “I have told you these things, so that in me you may have peace. In this world you will have trouble. But take heart! I have overcome the world.”

NRSVCE.1: “I have said these things to you to keep you from stumbling.
NRSVCE.2: They will put you out of the synagogues. Indeed, an hour is coming when those who kill you will think that by doing so they are offering worship to God.
NRSVCE.3: And they will do this because they have not known the Father or me.
NRSVCE.4: But I have said these things to you so that when their hour comes you may remember that I told you about them. The Work of the Spirit “I did not say these things to you from the beginning, because I was with you.
NRSVCE.5: But now I am going to him who sent me; yet none of you asks me, ‘Where are you going?’
NRSVCE.6: But because I have said these things to you, sorrow has filled your hearts.
NRSVCE.7: Nevertheless I tell you the truth: it is to your advantage that I go away, for if I do not go away, the Advocate will not come to you; but if I go, I will send him to you.
NRSVCE.8: And when he comes, he will prove the world wrong about sin and righteousness and judgment:
NRSVCE.9: about sin, because they do not believe in me;
NRSVCE.10: about righteousness, because I am going to the Father and you will see me no longer;
NRSVCE.11: about judgment, because the ruler of this world has been condemned.
NRSVCE.12: “I still have many things to say to you, but you cannot bear them now.
NRSVCE.13: When the Spirit of truth comes, he will guide you into all the truth; for he will not speak on his own, but will speak whatever he hears, and he will declare to you the things that are to come.
NRSVCE.14: He will glorify me, because he will take what is mine and declare it to you.
NRSVCE.15: All that the Father has is mine. For this reason I said that he will take what is mine and declare it to you.
NRSVCE.16: “A little while, and you will no longer see me, and again a little while, and you will see me.”
NRSVCE.17: Then some of his disciples said to one another, “What does he mean by saying to us, ‘A little while, and you will no longer see me, and again a little while, and you will see me’; and ‘Because I am going to the Father’?”
NRSVCE.18: They said, “What does he mean by this ‘a little while’? We do not know what he is talking about.”
NRSVCE.19: Jesus knew that they wanted to ask him, so he said to them, “Are you discussing among yourselves what I meant when I said, ‘A little while, and you will no longer see me, and again a little while, and you will see me’?
NRSVCE.20: Very truly, I tell you, you will weep and mourn, but the world will rejoice; you will have pain, but your pain will turn into joy.
NRSVCE.21: When a woman is in labor, she has pain, because her hour has come. But when her child is born, she no longer remembers the anguish because of the joy of having brought a human being into the world.
NRSVCE.22: So you have pain now; but I will see you again, and your hearts will rejoice, and no one will take your joy from you.
NRSVCE.23: On that day you will ask nothing of me. Very truly, I tell you, if you ask anything of the Father in my name, he will give it to you.
NRSVCE.24: Until now you have not asked for anything in my name. Ask and you will receive, so that your joy may be complete.
NRSVCE.25: “I have said these things to you in figures of speech. The hour is coming when I will no longer speak to you in figures, but will tell you plainly of the Father.
NRSVCE.26: On that day you will ask in my name. I do not say to you that I will ask the Father on your behalf;
NRSVCE.27: for the Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and have believed that I came from God.
NRSVCE.28: I came from the Father and have come into the world; again, I am leaving the world and am going to the Father.”
NRSVCE.29: His disciples said, “Yes, now you are speaking plainly, not in any figure of speech!
NRSVCE.30: Now we know that you know all things, and do not need to have anyone question you; by this we believe that you came from God.”
NRSVCE.31: Jesus answered them, “Do you now believe?
NRSVCE.32: The hour is coming, indeed it has come, when you will be scattered, each one to his home, and you will leave me alone. Yet I am not alone because the Father is with me.
NRSVCE.33: I have said this to you, so that in me you may have peace. In the world you face persecution. But take courage; I have conquered the world!”

NET.1: “I have told you all these things so that you will not fall away.
NET.2: They will put you out of the synagogue, yet a time is coming when the one who kills you will think he is offering service to God.
NET.3: They will do these things because they have not known the Father or me.
NET.4: But I have told you these things so that when their time comes, you will remember that I told you about them. “I did not tell you these things from the beginning because I was with you.
NET.5: But now I am going to the one who sent me, and not one of you is asking me, ‘Where are you going?’
NET.6: Instead your hearts are filled with sadness because I have said these things to you.
NET.7: But I tell you the truth, it is to your advantage that I am going away. For if I do not go away, the Advocate will not come to you, but if I go, I will send him to you.
NET.8: And when he comes, he will prove the world wrong concerning sin and righteousness and judgment —
NET.9: concerning sin, because they do not believe in me;
NET.10: concerning righteousness, because I am going to the Father and you will see me no longer;
NET.11: and concerning judgment, because the ruler of this world has been condemned.
NET.12: “I have many more things to say to you, but you cannot bear them now.
NET.13: But when he, the Spirit of truth, comes, he will guide you into all truth. For he will not speak on his own authority, but will speak whatever he hears, and will tell you what is to come.
NET.14: He will glorify me, because he will receive from me what is mine and will tell it to you.
NET.15: Everything that the Father has is mine; that is why I said the Spirit will receive from me what is mine and will tell it to you.
NET.16: In a little while you will see me no longer; again after a little while, you will see me.”
NET.17: Then some of his disciples said to one another, “What is the meaning of what he is saying, ‘In a little while you will not see me; again after a little while, you will see me,’ and, ‘because I am going to the Father’?”
NET.18: So they kept on repeating, “What is the meaning of what he says, ‘In a little while’? We do not understand what he is talking about.”
NET.19: Jesus could see that they wanted to ask him about these things, so he said to them, “Are you asking each other about this — that I said, ‘In a little while you will not see me; again after a little while, you will see me’?
NET.20: I tell you the solemn truth, you will weep and wail, but the world will rejoice; you will be sad, but your sadness will turn into joy.
NET.21: When a woman gives birth, she has distress because her time has come, but when her child is born, she no longer remembers the suffering because of her joy that a human being has been born into the world.
NET.22: So also you have sorrow now, but I will see you again, and your hearts will rejoice, and no one will take your joy away from you.
NET.23: At that time you will ask me nothing. I tell you the solemn truth, whatever you ask the Father in my name he will give you.
NET.24: Until now you have not asked for anything in my name. Ask and you will receive it, so that your joy may be complete.
NET.25: “I have told you these things in obscure figures of speech; a time is coming when I will no longer speak to you in obscure figures, but will tell you plainly about the Father.
NET.26: At that time you will ask in my name, and I do not say that I will ask the Father on your behalf.
NET.27: For the Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and have believed that I came from God.
NET.28: I came from the Father and entered into the world, but in turn, I am leaving the world and going back to the Father.”
NET.29: His disciples said, “Look, now you are speaking plainly and not in obscure figures of speech!
NET.30: Now we know that you know everything and do not need anyone to ask you anything. Because of this we believe that you have come from God.”
NET.32: Look, a time is coming — and has come — when you will be scattered, each one to his own home, and I will be left alone. Yet I am not alone, because my Father is with me.
NET.33: I have told you these things so that in me you may have peace. In the world you have trouble and suffering, but take courage — I have conquered the world.”
